title: OpenAI’s former product chief and Sora head leave company
summary: Kevin Weil, the former chief product officer who later took charge of the OpenAI for Science project, announced Friday that he’s stepping down. Weil joined OpenAI nearly two years ago and previously steered product development at Instagram and Twitter as an executive at those companies.

title: Cognizant CEO’s 2025 realised pay increases 30% to 10.7 million
summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions chief executive Ravi Kumar S took home $10.7 million in realised compensation in 2025, a more than 30% increase compared with $8.2 million in 2024, according to the company’s filings with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

title: Mythos a serious threat but more will follow, Barclays CEO says
summary: Anthropic's frontier AI model Mythos is ​a serious threat ​to the global banking system, and ​it is likely to be followed by similar, even more powerful cyberthreats, Barclays chief executive C S Venkatakrishnan said on ‌Friday.

title: Nvidia rival Cerebras discloses US IPO filing as AI boom drives listings
summary: Cerebras Systems revealed its filing for a ​US initial public offering on Friday, ​bringing the Nvidia rival closer to the public markets as it seeks to ​tap into growing optimism around a broad revival in the listings market.

title: Apple defeats bid for new Apple Watch import ban at US trade tribunal
summary: A US trade tribunal ruled for Apple ​on Friday against a ​bid from medtech company Masimo to reinstate an ​import ban on the tech giant's Apple Watches.

title: White House and Anthropic CEO discuss working together amid rising fear about Mythos model

title: Meta targets May 20 for first wave of layoffs; additional cuts later in 2026
summary: The Facebook ​and Instagram owner will lay off about 10% of its global workforce, or close to 8,000 employees, in that initial round.

title: Slow revenue conversion to dog IT companies as AI queers the pitch
summary: Decline in annual revenue runs at even the biggest in Indian IT, such as Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and Wipro, despite healthy deal pipelines suggests persistence of both slow revenue conversion and discounts spawned by competition from AI, analysts said.
